item.description: Iranian singer Parastoo Ahmadi has been sentenced to 74 lashes for performing in a YouTube concert without a hijab — and social media users around the world are responding with outrage. Ahmadi streamed the virtual concert on her YouTube channel back in December 2024, but the Qom Provincial Criminal Court in Iran has now charged […]

item.title: Police divers discover £430k of cannabis in cave on Italian island
item.description: Italian police divers have discovered a stash of drugs hidden inside a sea cave accessible by a submerged tunnel on one of Italy’s popular summer island getaways.

item.title: What Was The “Jellyfish-Like” Drone Swarm The Downed F-15E Pilot Reportedly Saw Over Iran?
item.description: One source described the Strike Eagle pilot seeing a “minefield of drones” in the skies over Iran, before their jet went down.

item.title: Shootout traps tourists at Rio sunrise lookout
item.description: Dozens of tourists were trapped Tuesday at a popular sunrise viewpoint in Rio de Janeiro as a gun battle broke out between police and drug traffickers in the neighborhood below them, the second such incident affecting visitors this year.Above the favela, tourists who had gone to watch the sunrise from the Dona Marta lookout -- which offers a panoramic view of the city -- were terrified by the gun battle.

item.title: Italy announces release of Flotilla activists detained in Libya
item.description: ROME, June 23 (Reuters) - Italy's Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said on Tuesday two Italian pro-Palestinian campaigners detained in Libya had been released after spending about a month in custody.

item.title: Colombia national registrar says final count in presidential runoff is nearly identical to initial count
item.description: BOGOTA, June 23 (Reuters) - Colombia's national registrar on Tuesday said a final count of votes in this weekend's presidential race differed in just 0.003% of ballots from an initial count that gave

item.title: Japan's emperor and empress arrive in Belgium for state visit
item.description: The arrival of Japan's emperor and empress in Belgium marks a symbolic moment for two countries whose partnership stretches back more than a century and a half.

item.title: Guinness crowns the world’s loudest person at 122.4 decibels
item.description: That is in the noise range of a chain saw, a jet aircraft taking off and an ambulance siren at close range.

item.title: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
item.description: The scale and brutality of the sexual attacks is unprecedented and aimed at terrorising communities, the UN says.

item.title: Man wounded in police shooting near Frankfurt's main train station
item.description: Police shot and wounded a man near Frankfurt's main railway station on Tuesday after he attacked and injured an officer during a police check, authorities said. The man, who police said was armed with a sharp object, was taken to hospital.

item.title: Child Under 12 Euthanized in the Netherlands, Dutch Government Confirms
item.description: Dutch Health Minister Sophie Hermans revealed that the child was euthanized due to severe illness

item.title: Kenya halts construction of U.S.-backed Ebola facility
item.description: Kenyan Health Minister Aden Duale halted construction on a U.S.-backed Ebola quarantine facility after a judge found him in contempt of court.

item.title: Gunfight at the Veracruz Custom House led to six Medal of Honor recipients
item.description: Six U.S. sailors and a Mexican naval cadet matched their heroism in an alley.

item.title: Archbishop of Canterbury plants olive tree in West Bank
item.description: The Archbishop of Canterbury planted an olive tree on a Palestinian Christian farm in the occupied West Bank on Tuesday. Sarah Mullally visited the Tent Of Nations farm project south of Bethlehem and heard from Daoud Nasser, whose family has owned the farm for more than 100 years, about how it has been increasingly surrounded by settlements in recent years and subjected to attacks by settlers, Lambeth Palace said.

item.title: French lawmakers back autonomy plans for Corsica
item.description: The French National Assembly on Tuesday approved Corsica's request for greater autonomy. In the first reading, 271 members of the lower house of parliament backed a corresponding constitutional reform, with 202 votes against and 64 abstentions.

item.title: Kenya halts construction of disputed US-backed Ebola quarantine centre
item.description: Kenyan Health Minister Aden Duale on Tuesday halted the construction of a controversial Ebola quarantine facility intended for US citizens after being summoned by the country's High Court for defying a judicial order. Judge Patricia Nyaundi declined to impose a jail sentence but issued Duale with a "stern warning" for failing to comply with the court's directive, Kenyan media reported.

item.title: No fuel for sale as Ukraine's blockade of Russian-occupied Crimea takes effect
item.description: In the biggest city Sevastopol, one resident told the BBC he had bought a back-up generator but had nothing to power it with.

item.title: French lawmakers back autonomy plans for Corsica
item.description: The French National Assembly on Tuesday approved Corsica's request for greater autonomy. In the first reading, 271 members of the lower house of parliament backed a corresponding constitutional reform, with 202 votes against and 64 abstentions.

item.title: Kenyan authorities to charge students with murder in dorm fire that killed 16 girls
item.description: Prosecutors in Kenya have prepared murder charges against students accused of starting a dormitory fire in May that killed 16 girls at a secondary school, officials said Tuesday. The May 28 fire tore through a dormitory housing 202 students at the Utumishi Girls School in central Kenya, and students were forced to flee through a single doorway when the school matron failed to open an emergency exit. The suspects, who are being held in custody for a 21-day period of investigation, will be formally charged in court, Kenya’s Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions said.

item.title: What has Britain's likely next prime minister said about Trump?
item.description: Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next leader, hasn't said a lot about President Trump, but his few statements have been critical.

item.title: EU observers back Colombia vote count amid challenge to results
item.description: A European Union mission that observed Colombia’s presidential election underscored Tuesday the transparency and efficiency of the country’s vote count process, which President Gustavo Petro has repeatedly questioned after results did not favor his preferred successor. The independent mission deployed about 150 observers for Sunday’s runoff election, whose results show conservative outsider Abelardo de la Espriella leading by 1 percentage point, or nearly 251,000 votes, with all but a fraction of the votes counted.

item.title: Iceland kills first whales since 2023, resuming whaling
item.description: Iceland's Marine and Freshwater Research Institute has recommended that no more than 150 fin whales are caught in the 2026 season.

item.title: Rabies bites back: Europe warned of return of rabid animals
item.description: Dog owners in Europe are being urged to ensure their pets are vaccinated against rabies following the recent reemergence of the disease, which is usually fatal to humans if bitten by an infected animal. Researchers at France’s National Agency for Food, Environmental and Occupational Health Safety (ANSES) have found two strains of the rabies virus circulating in countries including Ukraine, Hungary and Slovakia.

item.title: UN adopts resolution to ensure perpetrators of crimes against peacekeepers face justice
item.description: The U.N. Security Council unanimously adopted a resolution Tuesday authorizing new steps to ensure that perpetrators of crimes against peacekeepers face justice. Nearly 1,100 peacekeepers from countries around the world have lost their lives in the line of duty and thousands of others have been injured since 1948, according to U.N. peacekeeping department figures.

item.title: European summers are getting brutally hot. So why is air conditioning so rare?
item.description: While nearly 90% of US homes have air conditioning, in Europe it’s around 20%, and some countries have much lower rates.

item.title: Oil tanker traffic through Strait of Hormuz hits highest level since conflict began but mines remain
item.description: Oil prices drop as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picks up amid U.S.-Iran negotiations, though mines remain in the central channel.

item.title: Russia Is Getting So Starved For Fuel That It May Start Putting Car Gas Into Planes
item.description: The fuel used for planes isn't the same as automotive fuel, but Russia may soon be shifting to the lower-octane gas for planes as supplies are running low.

item.title: Fire breaks out in Madrid skyscraper
item.description: A fire broke out at Madrid’s second-tallest building, sending plumes of smoke billowing above the Spanish capital.

item.description: Dettol is facing a boycott in China after an advert showed a man looking for a partner “not tainted by other men”.

item.description: By Jonathan Saul LONDON, June 23 (Reuters) - An evacuation plan to enable hundreds of ships with some 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Gulf to sail through the Strait of Hormuz is underway after Iran

item.title: Mexico’s cartel violence down during World Cup: analyst
item.description: The drop in homicides coincides with a pause in law enforcement action against cartel leadership.

item.title: Peru's Sanchez says he will not recognize results of presidential runoff
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Why you're seeing Norway's World Cup 'Viking row' all over the internet
item.description: If you’ve been online lately, chances are you’ve seen throngs of Norwegian soccer fans mimicking the movement of oarsmen in a synchronized celebration that has quickly become a viral sensation.

item.title: UN maritime agency to evacuate 11,000 seafarers from Middle East
item.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) will evacuate thousands of sailors from the Middle East following the agreement between the United States and Iran to end hostilities and open the Strait of Hormuz. "We will begin the implementation of the evacuation plan for over 11,000 seafarers still stranded in the region," IMO Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez said on Tuesday.

item.title: Lebanon, Israel hold fifth round of US-mediated talks
item.description: Representatives of Lebanon and Israel are meeting once again in the United States as part of efforts to secure a lasting ceasefire. The fifth round of negotiations opened in Washington on Tuesday, the Lebanese news agency NNA reported.

item.title: Stampede at World Cup match viewing in Jordan's Amman kills 1
item.description: A stampede at a gathering of World Cup fans in Jordan's capital early Tuesday left one person dead and eight others injured, the state news agency said. Thousands of spectators had turned out in central Amman to watch the match between Algeria and Jordan on giant screens. The crowd, at Hashemite Plaza, grew and nine people injured in the crush were hospitalized, the Jordan News Agency reported, citing the Public Security Directorate.

item.title: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
item.description: Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation.

item.title: AI lawyer wins UK court case in historic first
item.description: ‘The potential this unlocks for access to justice in civil claims that would otherwise not be worth litigating is immense and exciting,’ lawyer says

item.title: Iran to hold Khamenei burial on July 9, four months after death
item.description: The funeral of Iranian supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in an Israeli airstrike on February 28, will take place on July 9 in his birthplace in north-east Iran. Khamenei will be buried in the city of Mashhad beside the shrine of the eighth Shiite imam, Imam Reza, Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref announced on Tuesday, according to the state news agency IRNA.

item.title: Estonian President Karis rules out second term for personal reasons
item.description: Estonian President Alar Karis has announced he will not stand for a second term, citing deeply personal reasons for a decision he said he had taken long ago. The 68-year-old made the announcement in a speech at an Estonian national holiday event in Rapla on Tuesday, later confirming to Estonian media that he had made the decision some time ago.

item.title: Tech Leaders Warn of the Human Cost of the Internet’s ‘Largest Social Experiment’
item.description: At Cannes Lions, technology executives made the case for a healthier internet for young people—and all of us.

item.title: Less Than A Quarter Of People Outside The U.S. Trust Trump
item.description: Trump’s approval rating internationally is significantly lower than his average rating in the U.S.

item.title: Suspect in Montreal officer's death left manifesto tied to incel ideology, source says
item.description: The suspect accused of killing a police officer outside a Montreal hotel had a manifesto that links him to an online community of frustrated men who talk about attacks against people who have sex, an official familiar with the matter confirmed Tuesday. The official, speaking on condition of anonymity as they were not authorized to speak publicly about the case, said the manifesto links the suspect to the “involuntary celibate” or “incel” ideology, a mostly online group of people, primarily men, who believe society unjustly denies them sexual or romantic attention. The manifesto prompted a warning to police forces across Canada about the possibility of copycat attacks against police officers.

item.title: Hospital worker in Hungary suspected of eating stolen body parts
item.description: Police in Hungary said on Tuesday that they are investigating a hospital porter on suspicion of collecting and storing body parts in his flat for his own consumption. The alleged case of cannibalism by the 30-year-old man is said to have occurred in the capital, Budapest.

item.title: Former Pinochet agents convicted over 1976 Washington DC carbomb murder
item.description: Attack targeted former Chile ambassador Orlando Letelier and his US colleague Ronni Karpen Moffitt

item.title: U.N. says Israel is intentionally targeting Palestinian children
item.description: A U.N. human rights committee said that Israel is committing genocide in the Gaza Strip and is deliberately targeting Palestinian children.

item.title: South African police investigate murder amid rise in anti-migrant attacks
item.description: The rise in anti-migrant attacks in South Africa has already left several people dead, with hundreds trying to leave.

item.title: Watch: Monkey briefly escapes enclosure at Mexican zoo
item.description: A city in Yucatan, Mexico, said a capuchin monkey briefly escaped from a local zoo while its enclosure was being cleaned.

item.title: 10 years later, a majority of Britons say "Brexit" was a mistake
item.description: Most Britons say leaving the European Union was a mistake and they'd favor a new referendum, but politicians have little appetite to reopen the wound.

item.title: Shootout traps tourists at Rio sunrise lookout
item.description: Dozens of tourists were trapped Tuesday at a popular sunrise viewpoint in Rio de Janeiro as a gun battle broke out between police and drug traffickers, in the second such incident to affect visitors to the city this year.Above the favela, tourists who had gone to watch the sun rise from the Dona Marta lookout -- a popular spot for its panoramic view of the city -- were left terrified by the gun battle.

item.title: Jordan resumes executions after nearly a decade with six hangings
item.description: Jordan has executed six convicted criminals for the first time in nearly a decade, Human Rights Watch reported on Tuesday. “Carrying out six executions in a single morning marks a sharp return to a practice Jordan has used only sporadically since reinstating capital punishment 12 years ago,” said Adam Coogle, deputy Middle East director at Human Rights Watch.

item.title: Lebanon: More killed as Israel says it targets fighters near Nabatiyeh
item.description: Two people were killed and another was wounded after Israeli forces opened fire on the southern Lebanese town of Nabatiyeh al-Fawqa on Tuesday, according to Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health. The ministry's Emergency Operations Centre said in a statement that Israeli gunfire targeting the town resulted in two fatalities and one injury.

item.title: LEGO's Julia Goldin named first-ever European CMO of the Year at Cannes Lions
item.description: Chosen by more than 800 marketing leaders from across Europe, Julia Goldin was recognised for helping transform LEGO into a global cultural brand through partnerships spanning entertainment, gaming and sport.View on euronews

item.title: Dubai opens prequalification for Gold Line metro
item.description: The Gold Line will start at Al-Ghubaiba in Bur Dubai and end at Jumeirah Golf Estates.

item.description: THE US air force pilot shot down over Iran and then rescued in a special forces operation reported seeing a “jellyfish” formation of Iranian drones.

item.title: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
item.description: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

item.title: More than 5,300 people still held in Myanmar scam centres: rights group
item.description: A UN report found the facilities are operated by foreign nationals who were trafficked by criminal gangs.

item.title: Concerns Grow After Magician Vanishes Without a Trace Amid Bizzare Message on His Website
item.description: A magician has gone missing in Queensland, Australia in strange circumstances, with cops now asking for military assistance to help track him down. Daniel Hidden, 26, was last seen in the early hours of June 14, leaving his home at around 3 a.m. According to a statement shared by the Queensland Police, “Daniel’s family hold serious […]

item.title: Watch: Stuck deer rescued from metal fence in Alberta
item.description: Firefighters, parks officials and animal rescuers teamed up in Alberta to rescue a deer stuck between the bars of a metal fence.

item.title: Turkey detains over 200 suspects, including alleged ISIS militants, in sweeping raid ahead of NATO summit
item.description: Turkey detained more than 200 suspects, including alleged ISIS militants, in a sweeping Ankara raid ahead of the July NATO summit Trump is expected to attend.

item.title: China’s newest aircraft carrier sails through the Taiwan Strait
item.description: China’s newest and most powerful of its three aircraft carriers sailed through the Taiwan Strait on Tuesday, the Taiwanese defense ministry said, a day after Taiwan began a five-day military exercise on responding to a Chinese attack. The Fujian carrier first sailed through the narrow body of water separating China and Taiwan in a trial run last September. It later transited the strait for the first time as a fully commissioned military vessel in December. China claims the self-governed island as part of its territory and has not ruled out the use of force to bring it under Beijing's control.

item.title: Frustrating Israel, fiber-optic killer drone technology has arrived in southern Lebanon
item.description: Analysts believe that any military engaged in conflict will, in one form or another, encounter technologies first seen in Ukraine.

item.title: UN estimates $1.38 billion in building damage across southern Lebanon
item.description: More than 11,000 buildings have been destroyed across southern Lebanon, with direct damage to buildings estimated at more than $1. 38 billion, according to a new rapid assessment released by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) on Tuesday.

item.title: Taco Bell is now offering ‘emotional support tacos’
item.description: The L.O.C.O.S. platform was launched to coincide with the World Cup but will extend beyond the event for fans who are either celebrating or need support.

item.title: Poland-Ukraine row is 'not helpful' and plays into Putin's hands, Brussels warns
item.description: Brussels has issued a stark warning as tensions between the Polish and Ukrainian presidents escalate. "There is only one happy observer in this type of situation, and that's the aggressor," the European Commission said.

item.title: Kenya's health minister orders suspension of construction on a US-backed Ebola facility
item.description: Kenya’s health minister on Tuesday ordered the suspension of the construction of an Ebola quarantine center for Americans, a day after he was held in contempt by a court that had halted the project. Trump administration officials had said that the United States was planning to send Americans who are exposed to Ebola while abroad to a new facility in Kenya instead of flying them home. In May, the high court had ordered the construction of the center to be halted pending a determination of the case filed by the Law Society of Kenya and the constitutional watchdog, the Katiba Institute, which argued that Kenya’s fragile health system was unable to handle a potential Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Leading Pakistan activist given life sentence over soldier's killing at rally
item.description: Human rights campaigner Mahrang Baloch denied inciting protesters before the official's death.

item.title: Iran’s stealth plan to keep control of the Strait of Hormuz - and why Trump can’t stop it
item.description: Iran has unveiled a plan to impose stealth fees on the Strait of Hormuz, using an insurance loophole to keep exacting tolls out of the key waterway post-war.

item.title: Australia's dilemma: Can beaches be made safe without killing sharks?
item.description: Crystal-clear water, barely a ripple, lifeguards on patrol - conditions at Sydney's famous Coogee Beach seemed ideal for a summer day by the sea. Then came the shock.

item.title: Chinese warships arrive in Russia as tensions simmer in Pacific
item.description: The partners have stepped up joint naval training and patrols in a challenge to U.S. military dominance in the Pacific.

item.title: Cyberattack hits state banks in Iran
item.description: Electronic banking services have been severely disrupted after another cyberattack on several state banks in Iran, Iranian media outlets reported on Tuesday. According to the reports, the three affected banks - Melli, Saderat and Tejarat - temporarily had to shut down their customers’ card services nationwide, meaning online payments were not possible.

item.title: Catholic rebel group days away from excommunication
item.description: Pope Leo XI faces an early test as a traditionalist Catholic group presses ahead with disputed bishop ordinations.

item.title: Teenage exposure to sexual content in TV and movies may lead to pornography use in adulthood
item.description: Media consumed in adolescence can have a major impact on sexual behaviours and expectations.

item.title: Irish government to contribute £197m to cross-border rail services
item.description: The money will come from the last allocation from the Irish government's Shared Island Fund.

item.title: Ukraine says it hit a railway bridge to Crimea, seeking to isolate the Russian-held peninsula
item.description: Ukraine said Tuesday its forces struck a railway bridge, a power plant and other key infrastructure targets in Crimea as Kyiv’s military authorities seek to isolate the vital Russian-held peninsula in the latest stage of the 4-year-old war. The drone attacks added to the woes on the Black Sea peninsula, where Russian authorities have had to suspend gasoline sales to civilians as Ukraine has intensified its recent campaign to disrupt supply lines and the electrical grid at the height of the summer tourist season. The peninsula was seized by force and illegally annexed by Moscow in 2014.

item.title: China's foreign minister calls on BRICS to strengthen strategic minerals cooperation
item.description: BEIJING, June 23 () - China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi called on BRICS countries on Tuesday to jointly respond ‌to global challenges from Ebola to AI, and ‌to strengthen cooperation on strategic mineral resources, according to a statement from ​his ministry. • Wang told a meeting in India that BRICS countries should respond to global energy and food security challenges and unite to respond to the Ebola epidemic in Africa.

item.title: Thailand Expands Crypto Mining Probe Into $300M Chinese Laundering Network
item.description: Thailand's DSI says a Chinese "grey capital" network used illegal crypto mining and cash mules to launder more than $300 million a year.

item.title: AI-generated clip falsely links Ugandan politician’s designer outfit to corruption raid
item.description: In May 2026, Ugandan security forces raided properties linked to former speaker of parliament Anita Annet Among as part of a corruption probe, seizing luxury vehicles. A video shared on social media claims to show a designer dress confiscated during the operation. This is false; the video is AI-generated. An AFP correspondent present during the raid confirmed that only vehicles were impounded.

item.title: Exceptional condition: World War II assault gun found
item.description: During construction work at Nordholz naval air base on the North Sea, workers unearthed an almost intact WWII StuG III assault gun that had lain hidden in the sand for 80 years.View on euronews

item.title: Oman and Iran to pursue talks on managing navigation in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: DUBAI, June 23 (Reuters) - Oman and Iran agreed on Tuesday to press on with discussions about the future administration of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, including maritime services in the

item.title: Iran says won't allow UN inspectors at bombed nuclear sites
item.description: Iran said Tuesday that the UN's nuclear watchdog will not be able to inspect key nuclear sites bombed by the US and Israel last year, as the first round of talks to end the Middle East war wrapped up in Switzerland.- Lebanon war - On the Lebanon front, a fifth round of negotiations between Lebanese and Israeli officials was scheduled to begin in Washington on Tuesday in a bid to end the Israel-Hezbollah conflict there.

item.title: Putin is under pressure as Ukraine menaces Moscow and chokes Crimea
item.description: Life has become increasingly difficult for ordinary Muscovites. Waves of Ukrainian drone attacks are now reaching the Russian capital with growing frequency, exposing the reality that the Kremlin’s supposedly impenetrable air-defence shield is anything but. Images of fires burning across Moscow have spread rapidly online, scenes that inevitably evoke memories of the Luftwaffe’s Blitz against London in 1940.

item.title: 'My sadness at giving up Guernsey's oldest pub'
item.description: A former landlord says it is likely the historic venue will become housing.

item.title: Venezuela needs justice, not just amnesty
item.description: Venezuela's National Assembly passed the Amnesty Law for Democratic Coexistence, presented as a step toward social peace and national reconciliation.

item.title: Germany battles aggressive outbreak of poisonous caterpillars
item.description: Authorities across Germany have been battling an aggressive outbreak of poisonous caterpillars, which can cause severe health issues and have led to the closure of parks and other outdoor spaces, including in Berlin.

item.title: An ancient battle feels closer than ever in a war-weary Lebanese city
item.description: Sweating under a powerful afternoon sun, the crowd slapped their chests in time with the chanter's cry, his mournful refrain echoing over the abandoned, rubble-lined streets of this wounded city that has been the focus of Israel's latest attacks in Lebanon. "Karbala, O Karbala!" the chanter called.

item.title: Russia nuclear bombers followed by NATO fighter jets off coast of Europe
item.description: Russia's Tu-160 heavy bomber, designed in the days of the Soviet Union, can carry both conventional and nuclear bombs.

item.title: Leader of Russia-backed Georgian region resigns to become Putin adviser
item.description: TBILISI, June 23 (Reuters) - The leader of South Ossetia, a breakaway region of Georgia that is backed by Russia, said on Tuesday that he is resigning to become an adviser to Russian President

item.title: India eyes a risky Everest mission to recover the body of "Green Boots"
item.description: A Mount Everest veteran tells CBS News why retrieving "Green Boots," whose remains have become a grim waypoint for climbers, would be a perilous mission.

item.title: Resurrecting Herodium: A royal desert fortress awakens After 2,000 years
item.description: Imagine standing in the heart of the Judean Desert, exactly where King Herod’s legendary palace once echoed with the sounds of a vibrant empire. This week, for the first time in two millennia, the ancient fortress of Herodium truly came back to life.

item.title: Iran says card-based banking hit by cyberattack on three lenders
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Ukraine's Kryvyi Rih comes under Russian missile fire - three dead
item.description: At least three people have been killed by Russian missile fire on the south-eastern Ukrainian industrial city of Kryvyi Rih, military administration chief Oleksandr Vilkul said on Tuesday. A further 19 people were injured, five of them seriously, Vilkul said on Telegram.

item.title: Israeli fire kills two in Lebanon as Hezbollah slams truce ‘violation’
item.description: Lebanese armed group Hezbollah denounced the ‘treacherous attack’, saying it was a ‘blatant’ violation of the truce.

item.title: Kenya minister stops construction of US-backed Ebola quarantine facility
item.description: The minister was found in contempt of court for allowing work to continue.

item.title: China Cuts 12,200 University Programs, Replaces Many With AI Degrees
item.description: China is eliminating 12,200 university programs while launching over 10,000 AI-focused degrees in a sweeping effort to align higher education with economic priorities.

item.title: Two killed in Israeli attack near Nabatiyeh despite ceasefire
item.description: Two people were killed and another was wounded after Israeli forces opened fire on the southern Lebanese town of Nabatiyeh al-Fawqa on Tuesday, according to Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health. The ministry's Emergency Operations Centre said in a statement that Israeli gunfire targeting the town resulted in two fatalities and one injury in the final toll.

item.title: Thai woman faces a Myanmar court in an immigration trial tied to US diplomat’s killing
item.description: A Thai woman appeared in a Myanmar court on Tuesday in her trial on an immigration-related charge linked to allegations that she killed her ex-husband, a U.S. diplomat, according to an attorney familiar with her case. Pavinee Supasirivisan is also charged with murder for the May killing of the diplomat, whose identity has not been released, but is first standing trial on a charge of violating Myanmar's immigration code, which applies to any foreign national who commits a crime there. Three prosecution witnesses, including immigration officers, testified during the hearing at Kamayut Township Court, the second in her trial, according to the attorney who spoke on condition of anonymity to avoid possible repercussions from Myanmar’s military-run government.

item.title: China rapidly catching up with US in biotech
item.description: The US was unambiguously the world’s biotech leader until a decade ago, but China has made huge investments.

item.title: A legal battle over a former Zambian president's burial might be over
item.description: A legal battle over where former Zambian President Edgar Lungu's remains will be buried may be over, more than a year after he died, as South Africa's Supreme Court of Appeal ruled Tuesday in favor of his family and rejected the Zambian government's claim of custody over his body. The ruling overturned a South African court decision that ordered the family to hand over Lungu's remains to the Zambian government for repatriation. Lungu died in South Africa on June 5, 2025, at age 68.

item.title: Zambia ex-president's family wins latest legal battle over what should happen to his body
item.description: Edgar Lungu's family and the Zambian government remain in dispute 12 months after he died.

item.title: Australia Sees 3 Shark Fatalities in 30 Days, Far Above Normal
item.description: For the third time in less than one month, a fatal shark attack occurred off the Australian coast. Shark attacks on the coast of Australia are not unheard of, with an average of 27 shark incidents each year, according to the Taronga Conservation Society Australia. However, the fatality rate is much lower at just 2.7

item.title: An Amazon Driver Made Over $400,000 Helping Students Cheat. Now He’s Going to Jail
item.description: A Liverpool, England, man who worked as an Amazon Flex delivery driver has been jailed after prosecutors said he made more than $400,000 (around £300,000) by helping university students cheat. According to the Crown Prosecution ... Read more

item.title: Niger becomes the third country to leave the International Criminal Court
item.description: Niger has formally left the International Criminal Court, accusing the judicial body in The Hague of selective justice. The west African country submitted a letter to the United Nations on Monday, triggering the withdrawal process from the court’s foundational treaty, the Rome Statute. Niger, Mali and Burkina Faso announced last year that they would leave the court.

item.title: Philippines temporarily blocks gaming app used by suspect in deadly school shooting
item.description: Philippine authorities said Tuesday they will temporarily block an online gaming app that one of two students blamed for a deadly school shooting has avidly used, to assess whether it played a role in fostering such violence. Three students were killed and 20 others were wounded when the two suspects, aged 14 and 15 and armed with a handgun each, opened fire Monday at the San Jose National High School in central Tacloban city. The Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center said its decision to block Gorebox was prompted by an ongoing police investigation which showed that one suspect was an avid user of the app, adding that this will allow authorities to determine “whether the platform played any role in the actions of the suspects.”

item.title: Kenya halts US Ebola facility: health minister tells court
item.description: Kenya has ordered a halt to preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility, the health minister told a court Tuesday, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous stop-work order.On Monday, the court said health minister Aden Duale was held in contempt for failing to respond to the order.

item.title: UK government plans new rules to tackle illegal deforestation
item.description: LONDON, June 23 () - Britain is planning new rules to ensure that everyday products ‌sold in the country, including coffee and ‌cocoa, do not contribute to illegal deforestation globally, the government ​said on Tuesday. Here are the details: • Businesses trading in commodities sourced from rainforests, such as soy and rubber, will need to check that their supply ‌chains are not ⁠contributing to illegal deforestation.

item.title: India’s three new naval ships boost maritime firepower
item.description: The country has made strides in constructing complex naval platforms but remains dependent on foreign suppliers for key technologies.

item.title: Drone operators aren't spared from the horrors of war, and they're top targets
item.description: Ukraine's soldiers aren't necessarily safer than other soldiers. They're seen as Russia's primary target due to their battlefield impact.

item.title: China shows aircraft carrier’s “dangerous” encounter with Japanese warship
item.description: China accused Japan of provocative maneuvers during the carrier group's deployment.

item.title: China overtakes West in race to build most powerful supercomputer
item.description: China has overtaken the US in building the world’s most powerful computer with a breakthrough machine.

item.title: How a giant ‘disco ball’ could turn Kazakhstan into an unlikely AI superpower
item.description: IN FOCUS: The president of the former Soviet Republic describes artificial intelligence adoption as a ‘matter of national survival’. Anthony Cuthbertson reveals why Kazakhstan is going all in on AI

item.title: WRC secures $6m back pay for Target garment workers in Guatemala
item.description: The Worker Rights Consortium (WRC) worked with a Target supplier to secure nearly $6m in wages to more than 750 garment workers in Guatemala.

item.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
item.description: Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham".

item.title: Kazakhstan seeks 'much more' in ties with EU as Tokayev visits Brussels
item.description: With Tokayev’s strategic visit to Brussels under way, Kazakhstan's ambassador to the EU tells Euronews in an exclusive interview that Astana is seeking even closer ties with the 27-member bloc, highlighting energy, critical minerals and AI ambitions.

item.title: 40 mayors worldwide endorse a pact to shape data center development
item.description: Forty mayors from around the world have signed onto a pact announced Tuesday to try to shape how urban data centers are built and operated. It's their vision for how urban data center development can be done sustainably — and not at the expense of their cities' natural resources, energy prices or climate targets. C40 Cities, an alliance of nearly 100 cities seeking to impact climate change, launched it during London Climate Action Week.

item.title: One killed in fan rush in Jordan after World Cup loss to Algeria
item.description: One person died of injuries sustained during a stampede by football fans in Amman following the Jordanian national football team's 2-1 World Cup defeat by Algeria. The Interior Ministry in the Jordanian capital said nine people were hospitalized after watching the game in San Francisco on a large screen at a public square in the capital.

item.title: One person killed in Berlin as two cars collide and hit tram stop
item.description: A man waiting at a tram stop in north-eastern Berlin was killed when two cars crashed near the stop and were hurled onto the platform as a result on Tuesday, according to the police and fire brigade. The man was struck and killed by a pole that had been knocked over by one of the cars involved in the accident, spokesmen for the police and fire brigade said.

item.title: South Sudan sets December date for long-delayed first-ever election
item.description: Plans could yet be scuppered again over logistical issues and continued tensions and violence.

item.title: Man jailed for life after restaurant stabbing
item.description: Dequarn Williams will serve a minimum of 19 years and five months for the murder last November.

item.title: Amnesty calls EU complicit in new Libyan crackdown on migrants
item.description: BRUSSELS, June 23 (Reuters) - Authorities in eastern and western Libya have intensified a crackdown on migrants and refugees in the last month with mass ‌arrests, detentions and expulsions, Amnesty International said on Tuesday, accusing the EU of ‌complicity in abuse. Libya, where control is split between rival western and eastern factions, has been a major transit ​route for people fleeing conflict and poverty towards Europe by the dangerous sea route since a 2011 NATO-backed uprising toppled

item.title: Netanyahu: Israel will remain in 'security zone' in south Lebanon
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) will continue to maintain its self-declared "security zone" in southern Lebanon, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Tuesday, despite the territory being a key point of contention in peace talks between the US and Iran. "The IDF will continue to act with determination in order to neutralize threats against our soldiers and our citizens, demolish terrorist infrastructure, and maintain the security zone in southern Lebanon," Netanyahu posted on X in a joint st

item.title: Tourists rinsed! Beachgoers charged to use shower for first time
item.description: British tourists could be made to pay to use beach showers at a popular Spanish holiday resort.

item.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak has most cases in first month of any African outbreak, WHO says
item.description: June 23 () - Congo's ‌Ebola outbreak ‌has the ​largest number of confirmed ‌cases ⁠in the first ⁠month of ​any ​Ebola outbreak ​in ‌Africa, a senior World Health Organization ‌official ​told ​a ​briefing ‌on Tuesday. (Reporting by ​Emma ​Farge and ​Ayen ‌Deng Bior;Editing ​by Alexander ​Winning).

item.title: Mystery of hit on Tren de Aragua leader: is it linked to US mining plans in Venezuela?
item.description: Trump boasted of assassinating Héctor Guerrero Flores but details are scarce and experts doubt it will harm drug trade

item.title: Rare School Shooting in the Philippines Renews Debate Around Prosecuting Young People
item.description: A shooting that killed three students and injured 20 others has renewed debate over the age of criminal responsibility in the Philippines.

item.title: German raids target five suspected Islamic State members
item.description: German police have searched the homes of five people suspected of being members of Islamic State, federal prosecutors said on Tuesday. No one was arrested during the raids carried out in the south-western town of Filderstadt, in Potsdam just outside Berlin and in the Limburg-Weilburg region in central Germany, the country's highest prosecuting authority in Karlsruhe said.

item.title: Netanyahu: Israel will remain in its 'security zone' in south Lebanon
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) will continue to maintain its self-declared "security zone" in southern Lebanon, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Tuesday. "The IDF will continue to act with determination in order to neutralize threats against our soldiers and our citizens, demolish terrorist infrastructure, and maintain the security zone in southern Lebanon," Netanyahu posted on X in a joint statement with Defence Minister Israel Katz and Chief of the General Staff Eyal Zamir.

item.title: Estranged husband of former Scottish leader sentenced to 5 years and 3 months in prison
item.description: The estranged husband of Scotland's former First Minister Nicola Sturgeon was sentenced Tuesday to five years and three months in prison for embezzling more than 400,000 pounds ($540,000) from the Scottish National Party to fund a luxury lifestyle when he was its chief executive. Peter Murrell, 61, admitted using party funds to buy a high-priced motorhome, a Jaguar electric SUV, luxury goods such as Bremont watches and household items that included two toilet seats. Sturgeon, who led Scotland's semiautonomous government for more than eight years, has distanced herself from Murrell’s crimes and says she was not aware of his crimes.

item.title: India 'proactively monitoring' Telegram over concerns about illegal content, government report shows
item.description: An Indian investigation found that Telegram is being used extensively to share child sexual abuse material and perpetrate financial scams, according to a report by the government, ‌which added it was "proactively monitoring" groups on the messaging app. The 35-page report by the Home Ministry's Cybercrime Coordination ‌Centre was submitted in court as part of the government's successful defence of a one-week ban of the app after a question paper to the country's ​medical school exam was allegedly leaked. In that case, Telegram was accused of not doing enough to prevent misinformation in its channels — a charge it denies.

item.title: ‘What ceasefire?’: In northern Israel, locals doubt an agreement can end the war with Hezbollah
item.description: In Israel’s northern-most town, Daniel Dorman knows his pizza shop will be mostly empty all day, just like it has been for weeks. A few customers dine at two tables in the corner. The rest of the restaurant, much like the town it’s in, is deserted.

item.title: Colombia will track cattle from origin to supermarket after beef was tied to illegal deforestation
item.description: More than 200,000 cattle came from protected areas between 2020 and 2024.

item.title: Armed group in Nigeria kills at least 20 people and exchanges gunfire with police
item.description: An armed group killed at least 20 people in a community located in the north-central region of Nigeria, police said Monday. The attack occurred Sunday in the Kawel community located in the Bokkos area of Plateau State, police spokesman Alfred Alabo said in a statement. Police officers quickly arrived at the scene and exchanged gunfire with the assailants, forcing them to retreat, Alabo said.

item.title: Afghan Taliban to hold rare, closed-door talks with EU officials on deportations
item.description: A delegation from the Afghan Taliban is traveling to Brussels on Tuesday for closed-door talks with European Union staff, expected to focus on deportations, said a Taliban official. Afghans make up one of the largest groups of migrants seeking asylum in the European Union, but a growing number of governments in the 27-nation bloc want to speed up and increase deportations for those whose claims are rejected or who commit crimes in their host countries. Afghan authorities have imposed draconian restrictions on rights, particularly for women and girls, since the Taliban seized power in the country in 2021 in the wake of the chaotic withdrawal of U.S.-led forces.

item.title: Former Scottish National Party chief jailed for five years
item.description: LONDON, June 23 () - Former Scottish National Party chief executive Peter Murrell was jailed for just ‌over five years on Tuesday after admitting ‌embezzling more than £400,000 ($540,000) of party funds to buy items such as ​cars, a motorhome and luxury goods. Murrell is the former husband of ex-SNP leader Nicola Sturgeon, who suddenly resigned in 2023, shortly before she was arrested as part of ‌the probe into ⁠the party's finances.

item.title: Second boy, 15, charged with murder over stabbing
item.description: Jamal Coombes, 17, was found fatally wounded during the early hours of Saturday.

item.title: Ukraine's drone pilot scoreboard logged over 800,000 recorded hits against Russia in half a year
item.description: The new tally means Ukraine has roughly doubled the rate of targets it's hitting since 2025, with almost 820,000 strikes reported in all of that year.

item.title: PM urged to help free jailed Iran pair before exit
item.description: The family of Craig and Lindsay Foreman fear they might be "lost in the hands of political change".

item.title: IDF expands control to approx. 70% of Gaza Strip as Southern Command prepares for next steps
item.description: Military officials say operational control could expand further while forces strengthen defenses and prepare for possible renewed fighting. The IDF has expanded its operational control to approximately 70% of the Gaza Strip, and defense officials believe the area under Israeli control could grow further in the coming months.

item.title: Israel’s deliberate targeting of Gaza children part of genocide: UN inquiry
item.description: United Nations Commission of Inquiry says Israel’s ‘deliberate targeting’ of Gaza children part of ongoing ‘genocide’.

item.title: One in five in Germany cannot afford one-week holiday
item.description: Summer holidays have become unaffordable for many people in Germany, with 21% of people not able to go on a one-week break, according to figures released on Tuesday. This amounts to 17.

item.title: Iran says 3,519 killed in US-Israel attacks since late February
item.description: Attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran have killed 3,519 people, Iran's judiciary spokesman Asghar Jahangir told state news agency IRNA on Tuesday, citing the latest figures from the forensic medicine authority. Jahangir did not specify a concrete timeframe.

item.title: 10 To 65 Year-Olds Banned From Sun Brollies On Italian Beach
item.description: The controversial move has left some locals incredulous.

item.title: Three found dead in Rhine river in south-western Germany
item.description: The bodies of three men have been recovered from the Rhine river in Germany after they had previously been reported missing near the south-western town of Biblis, police said on Tuesday. The men aged 23, 27 and 50 were found dead on Monday morning, police said in a statement, adding that there were no signs of third-party interference.

item.title: Kim Jong Un reaffirms North Korea nuclear buildup at party meeting
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un called for accelerating the expansion of North Korea's nuclear forces, citing U.S.-South Korea military cooperation, state media reported Monday.

item.title: Israel's 'deliberate targeting' of children part of ongoing Gaza 'genocide': UN probe
item.description: Israel is deliberately targeting Palestinian children in what has become a key factor in an ongoing "genocide" in Gaza, United Nations investigators charged on Tuesday, in a report slammed by Israel.This, it said, was a key factor in establishing "the genocidal intent of the Israeli authorities and security forces to destroy the larger Palestinian group in Gaza".

item.title: Exclusive: Downed US pilot reported seeing Iranian drones swarm in ‘jellyfish’ formation
item.description: A US fighter jet pilot rescued by special forces after being shot down over Iran in April described a shocking sight before ejecting from his aircraft: multiple Iranian drones hovering in the air, moving as one, in a formation that resembled a jellyfish, according to four sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Iranian president lands in Pakistan as US-Iran teams work to finalize a war-ending deal
item.description: Iran’s president arrived in Pakistan for talks Tuesday with officials who have been mediating negotiations between Tehran and Washington on a permanent end to the war in the Middle East, even as discrepancies emerged on what had been agreed so far and violence broke out again in Lebanon. President Masoud Pezeshkian’s visit to Islamabad comes as technical teams were working on details of the deal following high-level negotiations in Switzerland on Monday led by U.S. Vice President JD Vance and Iran’s parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf.

item.title: India’s youth are fuming. A Boston University graduate wants to fix that
item.description: After years of intense pressure to succeed, exam scandals and high unemployment, India’s youth are pushing for change.

item.title: Guinness crowns Canberra town crier as the world's loudest person at 122.4 decibels
item.description: Joseph McGrail-Bateup, an Australian professional air conditioner cleaner and honorary town crier, has been recognized as the world’s loudest person. Guinness World Records last week acknowledged the 58-year-old Canberra resident recorded the loudest ever shout by an individual.

item.title: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
item.description: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

item.title: Iran war day 116: US eases Iran sanctions; Lebanon ceasefire holds
item.description: US eases oil sanctions for 60 days after Iran agrees to allow international nuclear inspections.

item.title: Millions have spare rooms as Europe's housing crisis grows. Which countries lead?
item.description: One in three people in the EU live in under-occupied homes, while housing advocates say policymakers need to expand the supply of affordable, smaller properties.View on euronews

item.title: Reports: Renewed Ukrainian attacks on the Crimean Peninsula
item.description: Ukraine has attacked targets including oil facilities and a port in Russia's Black Sea region of Krasnodar and on the Crimean Peninsula, according to media reports on Tuesday. The oil terminal and oil depot were near the large city of Kerch in eastern Crimea, the online portal Ukrainska Pravda reported.

item.title: Inside Ukraine's secret drone campaign destroying sites deep in Russia
item.description: Ukraine's strikes on refineries are no longer only producing dramatic images — they have disrupted a significant portion of Russian oil exports.

item.title: Turkey detains 209 in raids in the capital of Ankara ahead of July's NATO summit
item.description: Security forces in the Turkish capital carried out sweeping raids on Tuesday ahead of next month’s NATO summit, and detained more than 200 people with suspected links to extremist groups, including the Islamic State group. U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to join other leaders of the 32‑member alliance in Ankara for the July 7–8 summit. Turkey is planning strict security measures for the summit, including banning demonstrations and restricting access to roads leading to airports, as well as sealing off areas around the summit venue and hotels hosting delegations.

item.title: Sri Lanka troops to battle deadly dengue mosquitoes as cases rise
item.description: Sri Lanka is deploying the military to contain the spread of mosquito-borne dengue fever, as health authorities warned on Tuesday that hospitals are being overwhelmed with more than 1,000 admissions daily.The World Health Organization (WHO) has warned that dengue and other mosquito-borne viruses are spreading faster and further due to climate change.

item.title: AI visuals of jailed ex-Pakistan leader in hospital mislead online
item.description: Concerns about the health of Pakistan's jailed former prime minister Imran Khan have triggered a torrent of AI-powered misinformation online, with posts sharing inauthentic visuals of his repeated hospital visits. The government has not released any footage or photos of these tightly controlled trips -- and those circulating online contain mistakes that are hallmarks of AI fabrication.

item.title: Priced out of World Cup games, Mexican fans take celebrations into their own hands
item.description: The thunderous cry rings out over a crowd gathered in front of a television propped up on plastic tables and past a maze of vendors lining a bustling working-class neighborhood in downtown Mexico City. It echoes over fans across the Latin American nation, who roar as they watch Mexico's national team win another match in the FIFA World Cup with eyes glued to screens set up in plazas, below highway underpasses and tucked away in taco stands.

item.title: On This Day, June 23: Thai soccer team becomes trapped in flooded cave
item.description: On June 23, 2018, 12 members of a teenage soccer team and their coach became lost and trapped inside a flooded cave in northern Thailand.

item.title: Inquiry ordered after building fire kills 15 in north India city
item.description: Witnesses described people jumping from windows to escape, while rescue teams broke through a wall to save lives.

item.title: Hezbollah launches night operations to locate IDF senior command in southern Lebanon
item.description: An analysis conducted by the 146th Division found that Hezbollah is collecting intelligence on the location of IDF senior command in southern Lebanon through observation and radio activity. IDF sources warn that Hezbollah appears to be using radio activity to locate and target senior field commanders in southern Lebanon, in a manner that raises red flags and will require a change in operational patterns.

item.title: Europe Today: Starmer's resignation, Brexit and the Poland-Ukraine row
item.description: Keir Starmer announced his resignation as UK prime minister, while Britain marked 10 years since the Brexit referendum. Relations between Poland and Ukraine remained strained as Brussels welcomed Kazakhstan's president for talks on closer cooperation.

item.title: Islanders invited to take part in sea challenge
item.description: Les Bourgs Hospice is encouraging swimmers to get in the sea for the 30 Bays in 30 Days challenge.

item.title: Football offers escape for Gaza, but World Cup spirit dimmed
item.description: Palestinians in Gaza struggle to find World Cup joy amid displacement, power outages, and memories of previous lives.

item.title: Body of 36-Year-Old Model Found Inside Suitcase in Bogotá
item.description: Detectives are investigating hypotheses of murder and brutal death following the finding of a dead 36-year-old woman stuffed into a suitcase within an apartment rented through a short-term housing website,… The post Body of 36-Year-Old Model Found Inside Suitcase in Bogotá appeared first on Where Is The Buzz | Breaking News, Entertainment, Exclusive Interviews & More.

item.title: Kenya police violence victims say compensation promise a 'smokescreen'
item.description: Two years ago, Gen-Z protests marked a new era for Kenyan politics, but led to dozens of deaths, and devastated families are unimpressed with government promises of compensation.Memorial protests are planned on Thursday to mark two years since the country's biggest show of dissent, when Kenyans stormed parliament to protest new taxes amid wider anger over corruption.

item.title: Fears medieval crypt will be lost without repairs
item.description: The crypt of St John on the Wall in Broad Street dates back to the 14th Century.

item.title: Image of Perth event for murdered British teen is AI-generated
item.description: Footage of British police handcuffing a student who lay mortally wounded after being stabbed by a Sikh man triggered protests in early June, but an image circulating online of a gathering in Australia to "honour" the teenager is AI-generated. The image appearing to show a crowd paying tribute to Henry Nowak at Perth's Scarborough Beach contains visual errors that are telltale signs of synthetic content, and local authorities told AFP that no such event happened.

item.title: George Washington’s letter accepting British surrender to go on display in London
item.description: A letter signed by George Washington accepting British surrender and marking the beginning of the end of the American Revolutionary War has gone on display in London.

item.title: Congo says confirmed Ebola cases rise to 1,048, including 267 deaths
item.description: June 23 () - The ‌Democratic Republic ‌of Congo ​said late on Monday that ‌confirmed ⁠Ebola cases in ⁠the country ​had ​reached 1,048, ​including ‌267 deaths. On Sunday, the number of confirmed cases ‌surpassed ​1,000 ​for ​the ‌first time since ​the ​outbreak began.

item.title: AI-generated images falsely shared as evidence of Sri Lankan former spy chief's torture in police custody
item.description: Sri Lanka's opposition parties have accused police of torturing the country's former intelligence chief Suresh Sallay currently in custody for "aiding and abetting" the deadly 2019 Easter Sunday bombings that killed hundreds, but purported screenshots from surveillance cameras circulating online are not proof of the allegations. The images show signs they were created with AI. A government spokesperson has also said there is "no truth" to the claim.

item.title: Nato prepares for the Battle of the Baltics
item.description: As sunbeams pierce the storm clouds over Latvia’s coastline, the US navy Seabees are building the boat ramps that may one day be needed to counter a Russian invasion.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz's future is unsettled even as more ships venture through
item.description: Ship traffic has picked up in the Strait of Hormuz since Iran and the U.S. signed an interim deal to end a war that constricted global oil supplies and fueled inflation, but questions surrounding control of the vital waterway and whether vessels will be charged tolls to cross it could interfere with negotiations to forge a lasting peace. Tehran and Washington clashed over the Strait of Hormuz again this past weekend. Citing Israel's latest attacks on Lebanon, Iran declared that it reclosed the strait.

item.title: Russian Soldiers Are Revealing Their Locations To Ukrainian Fighters Posing as Lonely Women Online
item.description: Ukrainian resistance fighters are deploying CIA catfishing tactics to lure Russian soldiers into revealing strategic information.

item.title: Australia's coal and gas exports violate our human rights, group says in new UN case
item.description: The group says that it is unlawful for Australia to continue approving fossil fuel exports without protecting its citizens.

item.title: Haifa man sentenced to over eight years in prison for providing information to foreign agent
item.description: Dimitri Cohen was convicted of making contact with a foreign agent and attempting to provide information that could be used by Iran and its terrorist proxies during wartime. Haifa District Court sentenced 28-year-old Haifa resident, Dimitri Cohen, to eight and a half years in prison on Monday, after he was convicted of having contact with a foreign agent and seven additional offenses of attempting to provide information that could be used by the enemy, Iran, and its terrorist proxies, during war

item.title: Resident says busloads of gatherers hit a New Zealand beach, and 10,000 cockles were gone
item.description: "They advertise it as: Come to New Zealand for your free seafood buffet."

item.title: Marc Bloch, historian and Resistance hero, joins France's Pantheon greats
item.description: Historian and Resistance fighter Marc Bloch, who was tortured and executed by the Gestapo in 1944, will enter the Pantheon on Tuesday in a rare tribute reserved for France's most revered historical figures.He will become the first historian to enter the Pantheon, an honour reserved for the most exceptional figures from the worlds of politics, culture and science.

item.title: Fact Check: Jeff Bezos Did NOT Say Water Should Be Used To Fuel AI Rather Than For Human Consumption In Public Remarks At 2026 VivaTech Conference In Paris
item.description: Did Jeff Bezos say water should be used to fuel AI rather than for human consumption in public remarks at the 2026 VivaTech conference in Paris? No, that's not true: Lead Stories found no evidence that Bezos made that statement...

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows to accelerate military buildup
item.description: Kim Jong Un vowed to beef up North Korea's defence capabilities, citing military modernisation efforts by South Korea and the United States pushing the region "to the brink of a nuclear war", state media reported Tuesday.It quoted Kim as saying "the US and the ROK are pushing forward with the ROK's possession of a nuclear submarine while getting evermore undisguised in their moves towards the reinforcement and modernisation of armed forces in the region", referring to South Korea by the acronym

item.title: Latin America's resurgent right notches another win in Colombia
item.description: Populist leaders are sweeping into power across Latin America, with Colombian voters the latest to embrace hardline right-wing politics -- but will the trend last?Shops are closing because people can't survive like this," said Sandra Gutierrez, a 60-year-old Colombian voter.

item.title: UN chief warns AIDS progress at risk from global funding cuts
item.description: UN Secretary General António Guterres warned on Monday that decades of progress in the fight against HIV/AIDS could be reversed by sharp cuts in international funding. "AIDS-related deaths have been reduced by 70% since their peak in 2004, but that progress is now under threat in the face of severe funding cuts," Guterres said in a post on social media platform X.

item.title: How women steer youths away from gang violence in northeastern Nigeria
item.description: Women-led campaigns are helping curb gang violence by changing young people’s mindsets in conflict-scarred communities.

item.title: Fact Check: FAKE AI Video Shows Ribbon Cutting Ceremony For Fictional Asphalt Road In 'Mukhosransk' -- Russia's 'Nowheresville'
item.description: Does a real video show officials at a ribbon cutting ceremony for a new asphalt road in Mukhosransk, Russia -- a road which was really just a fake roll-up prop? No, that's not true: This video is AI-generated. It is...

item.title: Three dead in Montreal shooting, including police officer and suspect
item.description: A shootout Monday in the Montreal borough of Côte-des-Neiges left three people dead, including a police officer and the suspect, police said.

item.title: Germany's Pistorius warns social division threatens national security
item.description: German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius warned on Monday that growing social divisions and political polarization could undermine Germany's security, arguing that national resilience depends on more than military strength. "In a country where social cohesion is destroyed and polarization takes hold, there is no security - neither internally nor externally," Pistorius said during a lecture in Berlin.

item.title: How 100 Romanian hospitals switched to pen and paper to defeat a national cyber-attack
item.description: For four days, dozens of Romanian hospitals went offline, as cyber-experts sought to defeat the hackers.

item.title: Global Breathe Cities Network Set To Expand Following $45m Investment
item.description: The global Breathe Cities initiative to improve air quality and protect public health is set to expand, following a $45 million investment from Bloomberg Philanthropies.

item.title: Woman Relaxing in Park When She Feels an ‘Itch’—Horror at What She Sees
item.description: Her story horrified readers, with one declaring: "I'd be jumping into a bonfire."

item.title: Apple vulnerable in Tata cyber breach
item.description: India’s largest conglomerate, Tata is one of Apple’s most important manufacturing partners outside of China.

item.title: 2 killed, including police officer, in shooting in Montreal
item.description: The suspect was shot and killed "right away," according to police, and there was no immediate word on a possible motive.

item.title: Russia preparing hybrid attacks on NATO's eastern flank, intelligence warns
item.description: Latvian intelligence warns Russia is preparing military provocations against Baltic states or Poland, including drones and missiles, to pressure NATO.

item.title: Beit Shemesh protesters block roads, damage property, as police seek to reestablish order
item.description: The demonstrators set fire to dumpsters and damaged fences in the area where the protests occurred. Unruly haredi (ultra-Orthodox) protesters in Beit Shemesh blocked roads and caused damage to public property on Monday night, Israel Police announced.

item.title: Gunman kills a Montreal officer at a Hilton hotel before being shot dead, police say
item.description: A suspect armed with a long gun opened fire Monday at a Montreal hotel, killing a police officer before officers returned fire, killing him, police said. Police Chief Fady Dagher said a second officer was seriously injured in the shooting in the city’s Côte-des-Neiges neighborhood but is in stable condition. The chief said it was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer had been killed in the line of duty.

item.title: A brutal gang rape in India revives painful memories of 2012 Delhi assault
item.description: The case from Begusarai in Bihar comes close in brutality to that inflicted on a woman during a sexual assault in 2012.

item.title: UK considers forcing social media firms to prioritise trusted news
item.description: LONDON, June 22 () - Britain is considering forcing social media companies to prioritise what the government called trusted news sources as part of ‌its broader push to tighten regulation of the sector. The culture department ‌said on Monday it was considering requiring platforms such as Meta's Facebook, Alphabet-owned YouTube and TikTok ​to make content from public service media — including the BBC, ITV and Channel 4 — and other trusted news providers easier to find in users' feeds and searches

item.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
item.description: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.

item.title: UK considers forcing social media firms to prioritise trusted news
item.description: Britain is considering forcing social media companies to prioritise what the government called trusted news sources as part of ‌its broader push to tighten regulation of the sector. The culture department ‌said on Monday it was considering requiring platforms such as Meta's Facebook, Alphabet-owned YouTube and TikTok ​to make content from public service media — including the BBC, ITV and Channel 4 — and other trusted news providers easier to find in users' feeds and searches. The move comes a week after the government announced a ban on under-16s using most ‌social media platforms.

item.title: YouTube & TikTok Compelled To Boost BBC & ITV Content Under UK Government Plans
item.description: YouTube, TikTok, and other major video-sharing platforms will be told to carry public service content prominently under new legislation being drawn up by the UK government. British ministers are pressing ahead with a new Media Bill that will require YouTube to make it easy to discover content from the BBC, ITV, Channel 4, and Paramount-owned …

item.title: Shooting in Montreal, Canada leaves three dead including suspect
item.description: A midday shooting in Montreal killed three people on Monday, including a police officer, a civilian, and the alleged gunman, triggering rare alarm in the Canadian city. A shooting like this in Montreal is rare.

item.title: Police officer among 2 killed in a Montreal shooting. Suspect is also dead
item.description: Two people are dead, including a Montreal police officer, following a shootout with a suspect, who was also killed, on Monday afternoon in the city’s most populous borough, Côte-des-Neiges, Montreal police said.

item.title: Over 240 firearms seized since start of 2026, caches found embedded in civilian area, IDF says
item.description: IDF says more than 240 weapons have been seized in the West Bank since early 2026, including arms hidden in civilian sites, and alleged that terror groups are embedded in population centers. IDF operations in the West Bank have uncovered numerous weapons caches in civilian areas since the beginning of the year, the military confirmed on Monday.

item.title: IDF kills Nukhba terrorist who took part in holding Omer Shem Tov hostage after October 7
item.description: An additional IDF strike killed three other armed terrorists from Hamas' military wing, all of whom the military said had been "attempting to advance attacks against IDF troops. " The IDF and Shin Bet (Israel Security Agency) killed a Nukhba terrorist in the northern Gaza Strip on Saturday, who had taken part in holding Omer Shem Tov in Hamas captivity after October 7, 2023, the military announced on Monday.

item.title: Teenage girl on the way to a high school in Gaza killed in Israeli strike, relatives say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza (AP) — An Israeli strike killed a teenage girl Monday as she walked to her high school to take an exam in Gaza City, relatives said. Raghad Hassan Ashour, 16, was headed to the 11th grade test when the blast occurred in a busy street in the Rimal district, according to a relative, Jameel Ashour. The military said the strike targeted a Hamas militant, but that it was aware of a claim that an “uninvolved individual was harmed.”

item.title: Defeated Colombian leftist calls for calm after post-vote violence
item.description: Colombia's defeated leftist presidential candidate on Monday called for calm and distanced himself from post-election violence following his hard-right rival's victory."I wish to make a very cordial appeal for composure and calm," said defeated leftist candidate Ivan Cepeda, stressing he had not called for violent demonstrations.

item.title: Syria has made no progress on Sweida reintegration plan, UN says
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Fire at animation studio in India’s Lucknow kills at least 14
item.description: The blaze broke out in a commercial building with a library and animation studio, killing mostly college students.

item.title: Palestinian children ‘unprotected’ as NGOs forced out of Gaza and West Bank
item.description: Isreal is pushing humanitarian groups and rights defenders to scale down operations in occupied Palestinian territories.

item.title: Ebola cases rise above 1,000 in Congo, with more than 250 deaths
item.description: The number of confirmed Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has climbed to 1,003, with 254 deaths and 100 recoveries, officials said.

item.title: At Least 14 Dead After Fire as Some Students Were Seen Jumping from Window to Escape
item.description: “They could not escape from behind also because of the smoke,” said Brajesh Pathak, the deputy chief minister of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh

item.title: 2 killed Montreal shooting, including police officer; suspect 'neutralized'
item.description: A police officer and a citizen were shot and killed by an armed suspect in Montreal’s Côte-des-Neiges neighborhood, authorities said.

item.title: Three people killed in Montreal shooting, including police officer
item.description: A police officer, a resident and an alleged gunman were killed in a shoot-out on Monday in Montreal, Canada, authorities said. “It is with great sadness that we confirm the death of one of our police officers while on duty.

item.title: Ebola Cases Surpass 1,000 with Death Toll Over 250 as Officials Struggle to Contain Outbreak
item.description: Health officials face challenges with contact tracing as the outbreak continues

item.title: Two young children found dead in locked car during France heatwave
item.description: Two young children, aged two and four, were found dead inside their family's car in the southern French town of Carpentras on Monday. According to initial findings, the siblings had accidentally locked themselves inside the vehicle, the local prosecutor's office said.

item.title: Gofman takes Mossad by storm, as spy org. ramps up plans to topple Islamic regime
item.description: New Mossad Director Roman Gofman, the first outsider to lead the spy agency since 2011, is exploring new methods to topple Iran's Islamic regime. There is no question about it: Roman Gofman has taken David Barnea's Mossad by storm since taking over the spy agency on June 2.
